# GymBroFormCheck

## Project Structure

- `app/` - Streamlit frontend.
- `src/vision/` - Person A: computer vision and smoothing.
- `src/physics/` - Person B: biomechanics and classification.
- `src/analysis/` - Person C: DTW and reference templates.
- `src/ai/` - Person D: coaching feedback and prompts.
- `src/utils/` - Person E: video helpers and rendering.
- `data/uploads/` - uploaded user videos.
- `data/outputs/` - processed videos generated by the app.
- `data/reference_videos/` - gold standard reference clips.
- `tests/` - evaluation and automated checks.

## Run The App

Use Python 3.12 if possible. MediaPipe can be sensitive to Python version, so a project virtual environment is recommended.

```powershell
py -3.12 -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app/main.py
```

If Python 3.12 is not installed, install it first:

```powershell
winget install Python.Python.3.12
```

Then close and reopen PowerShell before creating the virtual environment.

The app can also be run without creating a new environment if the dependencies are already installed:

```powershell
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app/main.py
```

The old command below still works for now:

```powershell
streamlit run app.py
```

## Processing Notes

- Uploaded videos are saved in `data/uploads/`.
- Processed outputs are saved in `data/outputs/`.
- The first pose-processing run may download the MediaPipe pose model into `data/models/`.
- If processing fails because the video is unsuitable, try a clearer MP4 where the full body is visible, lighting is good, and the camera is not blocked.
